skills.name: rollout-risk
skills.description: Use when a SpecGate rollout or rollback gate evaluates release safety for migrations, public API changes, cross-service changes, flags, canaries, or operationally risky delivery.
skills.prompt:
# Rollout risk gate rubric

## Purpose
Judge whether the artifact gives enough release-safety evidence for a human or delivery reviewer to trust the rollout path. This is an evaluator rubric, not a rollout drafting workflow.

## Inputs to inspect
- Approved PRD/spec scope and non-goals.
- Rollout, migration, observability, and rollback sections when present.
- Delivery evidence, changed files, linked incidents, and external dependency notes.

## Pass conditions
Return pass only when the artifact names deploy order, compatibility constraints, guardrails, rollback/forward-fix decision points, owners, and observable success or rollback signals. Data migrations must describe backfill/cutover behavior and what cannot safely roll back.

## Warn conditions
Return needs_changes when rollout is plausible but lacks one or two reviewable details such as flag owner, alert threshold, migration order, support handoff, cleanup trigger, or residual-risk owner.

## Fail conditions
Return failed when the change can affect production data, public behavior, external consumers, or availability and the artifact has no credible rollout or rollback evidence. Treat unobservable rollback criteria, same-deploy destructive migrations, or unnamed safety owners as high severity.

## Output
Report verdict, highest risks, missing evidence, and concrete fix recommendations. Do not invent rollout steps that are not in the artifact; flag missing evidence instead.

skills.name: acceptance-criteria
skills.description: Use when a SpecGate quality gate evaluates whether acceptance criteria are observable, bounded, edge-case aware, and aligned with approved scope.
skills.prompt:
# Acceptance criteria gate rubric

## Purpose
Judge whether the artifact defines a verifiable definition of done. This rubric evaluates existing criteria; it must not expand product scope or write a new PRD.

## Pass conditions
Pass when criteria name actor or system trigger, starting state, observable result, and enough edge/failure behavior for the approved risk level. Criteria should separate user-visible outcomes from technical contract checks and point to explicit non-goals when useful.

## Warn conditions
Return needs_changes when the primary outcome is testable but supporting cases are thin: validation, permissions, empty state, failure recovery, auditability, or operational checks are missing for a risky flow.

## Fail conditions
Fail when criteria are mostly vague phrases such as works correctly, are not measurable, contradict approved scope, or cannot be mapped to tests/review evidence. Do not accept implementation tasks as a substitute for acceptance criteria.

## Output
Report verdict, missing or ambiguous criteria, edge-case gaps, and the smallest clarifying questions needed before implementation or delivery review.

skills.name: task-breakdown
skills.description: Use when a SpecGate gate evaluates whether an implementation plan is traceable, ordered, parallelizable, and verifiable against approved artifacts.
skills.prompt:
# Implementation plan traceability gate rubric

## Purpose
Judge whether the existing implementation plan is safe to hand to humans or coding agents. This rubric evaluates plan quality; it must not create a new task plan.

## Pass conditions
Pass when each major requirement maps to an owned task with lane, dependency, definition of done, and verification evidence. The plan should show contract-first ordering for FE/BE work, migration-before-use ordering for schema changes, QA coverage for acceptance criteria, and docs/update tasks when behavior changes.

## Warn conditions
Return needs_changes when the plan is mostly usable but has missing dependency labels, vague verification, unclear parallel boundaries, weak docs coverage, or one oversized task that should be split before handoff.

## Fail conditions
Fail when tasks do not trace to requirements, omit critical backend/frontend/QA ownership, hide migration or external dependency order, or use broad tasks such as implement feature with no observable completion evidence.

## Output
Report verdict, traceability gaps, dependency/order risks, missing verification, and recommended task fixes by section or task id.

skills.name: spec-review
skills.description: Use when a SpecGate readiness gate evaluates whether a technical specification is complete, internally consistent, secure, operable, and implementation-ready.
skills.prompt:
# Technical specification gate rubric

## Purpose
Judge whether the approved or proposed technical contract is executable enough for implementation and review. This rubric returns findings; it does not rewrite the spec.

## Pass conditions
Pass when requirements are testable, terminology is stable, interfaces/events/errors are concrete, data invariants and migrations are named when relevant, security and authorization behavior are explicit, and observability/rollout assumptions match the impact level.

## Warn conditions
Return needs_changes when the contract is directionally sound but leaves reviewable gaps such as unclear error shape, missing versioning stance, thin observability, undefined dependency limits, or incomplete migration detail.

## Fail conditions
Fail when ambiguity could lead to incompatible implementations, data loss, security/privacy exposure, undeclared breaking changes, impossible QA, or a mismatch between API, data, and acceptance criteria.

## Output
Report verdict, severity-tagged findings, affected spec sections, missing evidence, and minimal author questions. Keep product-intent issues scoped to whether they block the technical contract.

skills.name: prd-review
skills.description: Use when a SpecGate scope gate evaluates whether product intent, user value, non-goals, success metrics, and acceptance boundaries are clear enough for specification.
skills.prompt:
# Product intent gate rubric

## Purpose
Judge whether the product artifact gives enough intent and scope evidence for technical specification. This rubric evaluates product clarity; it must not design APIs, schemas, or implementation plans.

## Pass conditions
Pass when the problem, target users, goals, non-goals, core scenarios, measurable success signals, and product-level acceptance boundaries are explicit and mutually consistent.

## Warn conditions
Return needs_changes when the direction is clear but one reviewable dimension is weak: stakeholder boundary, guardrail metric, edge scenario, non-goal, or measurable success signal.

## Fail conditions
Fail when the problem is solution-first with no user value, scope can be interpreted multiple ways, success cannot be measured, compliance/business risk is unaddressed, or acceptance criteria would let the wrong product ship.

## Output
Report verdict, scope/metric/acceptance gaps, product questions, and any technical-spec topics that must be addressed later without designing them here.

skills.name: review-impl
skills.description: Use when a SpecGate delivery_review gate evaluates coding-agent completion evidence against the approved Context Pack, tests, docs, changed files, and policy obligations.
skills.prompt:
# Delivery review gate rubric

## Purpose
Judge whether implementation evidence satisfies the approved Context Pack and can move to completed delivery. This rubric reviews completion evidence, not pre-implementation planning.

## Inputs to inspect
- Approved Context Pack, spec, acceptance criteria, non-goals, and applicable policy obligations.
- Coding-agent delivery report, changed files, test commands and output, docs updates, linked PR/commit evidence, and any failed/waived checks.
- Gate results, reviewer feedback, artifact updates, and known risk/rollout notes.

## Pass conditions
Pass when implementation evidence traces every required acceptance item to changed files or explicit no-code rationale, required tests/checks passed with concrete command output, docs/spec updates are accounted for, policy evidence is corroborated as required, and no approved non-goal or scope boundary was crossed.

## Warn conditions
Return needs_changes when the work appears likely correct but evidence is incomplete: missing command output, unlinked docs update, unclear file-to-criteria traceability, stale Context Pack reference, unresolved reviewer comment, or non-critical rollout note.

## Fail conditions
Fail when required checks failed or were skipped without accepted exception, implementation drifts from approved scope, delivery evidence cannot be corroborated, security/privacy obligations are unmet, or docs/spec changes required by the code are missing.

## Output
Report verdict, evidence gaps, scope drift, missing tests/docs, and required next action. Do not invent passing evidence or treat an agent claim as proof without corroborating artifacts.
